old_claim.note: Electric Railway Signaling provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Hoge's municipal traffic-control patent explicitly treats electrically controlled railway-signal types as a design precedent for street traffic signals.

source_shape.source_locator: Patent specification says the street signal may use well-known electrically controlled signals used in railway signaling systems.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies railway signaling as a predecessor design repertoire for municipal street traffic signals.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Railway signaling informed the signal form, but the patent scopes the new system to municipal street and road traffic control, so lineage is more accurate than direct enabling.

ontology_before: Traffic Signal Systems treated Electric Railway Signaling as a generic enabling prerequisite for street signals.
ontology_after: Traffic Signal Systems now treats railway signaling as a source-backed historical design predecessor while direct electrical and road-intersection dependencies carry the mechanism.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed edge type: electric_railway_signaling is now historical_predecessor rather than enabling.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to railway crossing signals rather than municipal road-intersection traffic signals.
A stronger source shows the Cleveland/Hoge signal was a direct railway signaling installation rather than a street-traffic adaptation.
The graph adds a separate semaphore_traffic_signals node that better captures the railway-signaling lineage.
short_reason: Railway signaling was a design precedent, not the direct operating substrate.
